default: Let me explain the systems angle — measure first, then upgrade.
Good question — visibility on power, gas, and water usually comes before big capex.
consumer_overview: I cover monitoring, sensors, and how building systems behave for homes and restaurants.
Edwardo here — systems and equipment visibility, not just dashboards for their own sake.
monitoring_why: You cannot fix what you cannot see — here is why monitoring pays back.
Baseline measurement makes every upgrade and finance case stronger.
sensors_restaurant: Restaurants waste energy in peaks, overnight HVAC, and cold chain drift — sensors target those.
Cookline power, dishwash water, and refrigeration are usually the first meters worth adding.
sensors_home: At home, smart-meter data and a few circuit clamps often reveal the biggest surprises.
Heating, hot water, and standby loads are where home monitoring earns its keep.
sensors_office: Offices often bleed kWh after hours — schedule and submeter data expose it fast.
Tenant submetering and HVAC runtime are the usual office starting points.
monitoring_products: Here is how I think about monitoring products — granularity and alerts matter more than gadget count.
I will walk through review themes and the Greenways tools that pair with them.
deep_dive_systems: Deep dive applies the same €/kWh model to one asset — that is how systems savings become real numbers.
Compare your line to ETL alternatives in the decision matrix — I will explain what the dashboard would show after upgrade.
greenways_dashboard: Greenways has a buildings dashboard — KPI tiles, trends, and utility drill-down.
The embed is still being finalised for Render, but the maths and pages work — I can walk you through them.
dashboard_math: Cost on Greenways uses a shared site-energy model — I will spell out the formulas.
Baseline vs actual is how we spot drift before the bill lands.
time_of_use: Running the same kit at peak lunch hours costs more than off-peak — timing matters.
Shift batch cycles and HVAC pre-cool where you can; upgrade kit to shrink the baseline band.
etl_systems_savings: ETL is verified efficiency — the dashboard and deep dive translate that into €/day saved.
Real examples on Greenways show what organisations saved — we can map that to your site.
role_resources: These guides shaped how I advise on monitoring and systems rollouts.
I link facilities, finance, and sustainability through measurable data.
